behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: source collectors + aggregation/composition feeding the summary step. A pure Unit test of one formatter could miss wiring bugs (e.g., one source dropped). No real OS/network/subprocess is required if the three source clients are stubbed in-process, and correctness does not depend on model output content because assertion is on the pre-summary payload.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real data-collection orchestration with three in-process fakes (metrics, issue tracker, deployment log) returning distinct markers; execute report build up to the summary-call boundary and assert the exact payload/context sent to summary generation contains all three source sections/values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation/recovery: one fetcher fails, orchestrator continues, report assembler inserts section note. Unit is too low because the failure appears at handoff between fetch, fallback, and assembly. Higher levels were rejected: behavior text is about fallback control flow, not real OS/network semantics (System) and not model judgment quality (Agentic).
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two successful source fakes and one fake source raising an availability error; verify generation completes, report contains a `data unavailable` marker in the failed source section, and process does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on summary quality from a real model: factual preservation/omission/hallucination can vary across runs. A deterministic stub would always pass and hide real failures (deterministic-assertion trap). Workflow is not required because this behavior names one model judgment step (summary generation), not multiple agentic decisions chained.
behaviors.test_description: Use representative real input datasets with known key values, call the real summary-generation model, and score outputs for factual fidelity (key numbers/entities present and not contradicted) across repeated runs with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox, which crosses real network/email infrastructure and external service semantics. Integration fakes can miss SMTP/API auth, transport, MIME/header, and provider delivery behavior. Agentic was considered but rejected by behavior text: success criterion is inbox delivery correctness, not evaluating model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled report pipeline end-to-end against test instances of the three data sources and a real email delivery path to a test mailbox; assert message arrives in the configured inbox within SLA and includes the generated report body/attachments with expected identifiers.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode spans real infrastructure boundaries: unreachable delivery endpoint, local filesystem persistence, and next-run retry across schedule/run boundary. In-process mocks can hide real durability/path/permission/timing issues. No model-output-quality dependency is described, so Agentic/Workflow are not required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script with email endpoint intentionally unreachable (e.g., blocked host/port), verify report is written to disk at configured fallback path, then trigger the next scheduled execution with email restored and assert persisted report is retried and successfully delivered.
